text: So what was really exciting about this case was that we used a brand new 3D laparoscope which gives us excellent depth perception and increases the safety of the procedure because we don't have to constantly distance seek for our different instruments and for the different structures within the chest.
So this image shows the port position for thoracoscopic surgery.
And here we're performing a complete exploration of the thoracic cavity and we can see foam that's developed from the accumulation of pleural fluid due to the pericardial effusion.
At this point we're perforating the mediastinum from the contralateral side of the chest so that we can start the thoracoscopic pericardectomy.
We extend the division of the mediastinum cranially until we get to the point of the internal thoracic artery.
Here we're using the laparoscopic or thoracoscopic Metzenbaum scissors to make our initial perforation in the pericardial sac.
We use the monopolar electrocautery to continue the creation of the window and the pericardial sac.
And you can see that we've removed the majority of the ventral aspect of the pericardial sac using a combination of monopolar electrocautery as well as our ligasure unit.
Note that we are removing the pericardial sac dorsally to the level of the phrenic nerves, leaving those intact so as not to affect respiration.
